label: General small molecules
reason: Common starting point for routine reversed-phase small-molecule HPLC methods.

label: Routine QC
reason: 4.6 mm ID, 150/250 mm length and 5 µm particles are common starting points for robust routine HPLC methods.

label: Robust general purpose
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil ODS2 as a robust general-purpose C18 option with good performance for acidic, alkaline and neutral compounds and broad pharmaceutical, food, biological product and environmental examples.

label: Basic analytes
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil ODS-B as base-deactivated C18 intended to reduce silanol activity and support alkaline/basic compounds.

label: 100% aqueous / polar
reason: PDF guide states Supersil AQ-C18 is polar-modified for use in 100% aqueous mobile phases and enhanced retention/resolution of polar analytes.

label: Moderately polar analytes
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil C8 as lower hydrophobic retention than C18 and suitable for moderately polar analytes.

label: Bioproducts / insulin
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil C4 as based on 300 Å pore size and designed for separation of bioproducts, generally MW 10,000-100,000, with insulin/growth factor example.

label: Aromatics / pi-pi selectivity
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil Phenyl as providing enhanced pi-pi interaction with aromatics and high stereoselectivity.

label: Sugars / amino phase
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil NH2/NH2-S as aminopropyl columns compatible with reversed phase, normal phase and weak anion exchange, suitable for strong polar analytes and carbohydrates.

label: Strong cation exchange
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil SCX/SCX-2 as strong cation-exchange columns for alkali/cationic compounds, with 300 Å option for biological detection.

label: Strong anion exchange
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil SAX as a strong anion-exchanger for acidic and anionic compounds.

label: HILIC polar selectivity
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil Diol as a general HILIC mode column providing alternative selectivity to C18 for polar analytes.

label: CN complementary selectivity
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil CN as compatible with reversed and normal phase, offering complementary selectivity to C18, especially for hydrophobic analytes and carbonyl/amine compounds.

label: Normal phase / fat-soluble
arabic: طور عادي / مركبات ذائبة في الدهون
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil SiO2 as a general normal-phase silica suitable for strong polar and liposoluble analytes.

label: HILIC hydrophilic analytes
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil HILIC as providing enhanced retention of polar and hydrophilic analytes.

label: Oligosaccharides / amide HILIC
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil Amide as HILIC mode with selectivity for neutral, acidic and basic oligosaccharides and improved stability versus NH2.

label: Polar/basic aromatics
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil Polar-Phenyl as ether-linked phenyl with embedded polar groups for added retention of strong polar or strong basic analytes and improved peak shapes.

label: PFP / halogenated isomers
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil PFP as complementary to C18 with multiple retention mechanisms, suitable for aromatics, rigid ring systems and halogenated positional isomers.

label: Amino acids / peptides / vitamins
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil HILIC-ARG as providing selectivity for hydrophilic analytes such as amino acids, peptides, vitamins and nucleic acids.

label: Fat-soluble isomers / carotenoids
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil C30 as designed for high shape selectivity of hydrophobic structurally related isomers including carotene and fat-soluble vitamins.

label: Fast HPLC/UHPLC C18
reason: PDF guide describes ECoreshell C18 as core-shell C18 for high resolution/performance with low backpressure in HPLC/UHPLC systems.

label: Wide pH method development
reason: PDF guide describes SinoPak BEH T-C18 as a stable hybrid C18 for broad pH/temperature range and method development of unknown multi-component analytes.

label: BEH AQ-C18 / aqueous
reason: PDF guide describes SinoPak BEH AQ-C18 as embedded amide C18 compatible with 100% aqueous mobile phase and broad pH stability.

label: C8 quicker elution / isomers
reason: PDF guide describes SinoPak BEH T-C8 as less hydrophobic than C18, allowing quicker elution and higher stereoselectivity for isomers and analogues.

label: BEH phenyl aromatics
reason: PDF guide describes SinoPak BEH Phenyl/Phenyl-Hexyl as chemically stable phenyl phases with unique selectivity for aromatics and related compounds.

label: High-carbon C18 / complex samples
arabic: C18 عالي التحميل الكربوني / عينات معقدة
reason: PDF guide describes SinoPak C18 as high-carbon-load silica C18 for complex organic samples such as TCM, food additives, pigments and plasticizers.

label: AQ-C18 / medical drugs / peptides
reason: PDF guide describes SinoPak AQ-C18 as fully porous triple-bonded C18 compatible with 100% aqueous mobile phases, with examples in medical device drugs and peptide purification/preparation.

label: C8 moderately polar
reason: PDF guide describes SinoPak C8 as a dense bonded C8 for moderately polar analytes.

label: Phenyl aromatic selectivity
reason: PDF guide describes SinoPak Phenyl/Phenyl-Hexyl as phenyl phases with stereoselectivity for aromatic analytes and broad drug/food/environment applications.

label: BDS C18 for basic/polar analytes
reason: PDF guide describes Hypersil BDS C18 as base-deactivated silica suitable for alkali compounds and widely used for polar analytes.

label: BDS C8 polar analytes
reason: PDF guide describes Hypersil BDS C8 as base-deactivated C8 suitable for moderately or strongly polar analytes.

label: BDS CN polar / normal phase
reason: PDF guide describes Hypersil BDS CN as base-deactivated cyano suitable for moderately/strongly polar analytes and non-polar compounds in normal phase.

label: BDS phenyl aromatics
reason: PDF guide describes Hypersil BDS C6H5 as base-deactivated phenyl suitable for aromatic analytes and non-polar normal-phase work.

label: Classical ODS2 methods
reason: PDF guide describes Hypersil ODS2 as a classical global-standard C18 with excellent stability/reproducibility for many existing methods.

label: Classical ODS methods
reason: PDF guide describes Hypersil ODS as a classical C18 with low carbon load and quick elution for weak polar analytes.

label: Classical C8 moderately polar
reason: PDF guide describes Hypersil C8 as suitable for moderately or stronger polar analytes with good peak shapes for acidic, basic and neutral compounds.

label: Water-soluble vitamins
reason: PDF guide describes Hypersil C1 as suitable for moderately polar analytes or compounds with multiple functional groups, with water-soluble vitamin application.

label: Classical phenyl aromatics
reason: PDF guide describes Hypersil C6H5 as a classical phenyl phase with stereoselectivity for aromatic analytes.

label: Silica normal phase vitamins/pesticides
reason: PDF guide describes Hypersil SiO2 as normal phase silica suitable for strong polar and fat-soluble analytes, including vitamins and pesticides.

label: CN double/triple-bond selectivity
reason: PDF guide describes Hypersil CN as compatible with normal and reversed phases and suitable for compounds containing double or triple bonds.

label: Classical NH2 carbohydrates
reason: PDF guide describes Hypersil NH2/NH2-S as compatible in normal/reversed phases and weak anion exchange, with carbohydrate examples.

label: SAX pesticides / glyphosate
reason: PDF guide describes Hypersil SAX as a quaternary amine phase used for active groups and pesticide examples including glufosinate/glyphosate.

label: Protein/peptide reversed phase
reason: PDF guide describes SinoChrom ODS-AP as 300 Å C18 suitable for biological products such as peptides and proteins, including salmon calcitonin and insulin.

label: Hydrophilic samples / high aqueous
arabic: عينات محبة للماء / محتوى مائي عالي
reason: PDF guide describes SinoChrom ODS-BP as stable in high-aqueous mobile phases and suitable for hydrophilic samples, TCM, chemical/pharmaceutical intermediates and foods.

label: C8 hydrophobic / bioproducts
reason: PDF guide describes SinoChrom C8 as recommended for high hydrophobic samples, with 300 Å option for biological products such as peptides and proteins.

label: C4 biological products
reason: PDF guide describes SinoChrom C4 as recommended for biological products with 300 Å pore size for peptides and proteins.

label: SinoChrom silica pesticides/vitamins
reason: PDF guide describes SinoChrom SiO2/Si60 as rugged unbonded silica options with examples in pesticides, peptides/lactoferrin and vitamin E isomers.

label: Fast core-shell general
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil Coreshell C18/C18 Plus as core-shell columns for high resolution and low backpressure, suitable for acids, bases and neutrals.

label: Hydrophobic compounds / steroids / fats
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil Coreshell C8 as moderate hydrophobicity/spatial selectivity with applications in hydrophobic compounds, moderately polar compounds, steroids and fats.

label: Core-shell phenyl aromatics / aqueous
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil Coreshell PH/PH Plus as phenyl core-shell phases for aromatics and compatibility with 100% aqueous mobile phase systems.

label: Special selectivity / isomers
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil Coreshell BP/PFP/CN as special-selectivity core-shell phases for aromatics, halogenated/isomeric compounds, strong polar compounds and related drug work.

label: Core-shell bio macromolecules
reason: PDF guide describes Supersil Coreshell BIO phases as 300/1000 Å columns for proteins, peptides, amino acids, hormones and macromolecular compounds.

label: Organic SEC/GPC polymers
reason: PDF guide describes Amber GPC as PS/DVB organic SEC/GPC columns suitable for many organic solvents and polymer/resin molecular weight work.

label: Aqueous SEC biomolecules
reason: PDF guide describes Amber SEC/SEC Plus/SEC 3 µm/PMMA SEC families for biological molecules, hydrosoluble polymers, proteins, antibodies, vaccines and nanoparticles.

label: Sugar / organic acids / fermentation
reason: PDF guide describes Amber Sugar H/Ca/Pb/K/Na columns for fermentation products, fruit juices, organic acids, alcohols, sugars, corn syrup, dairy sugars, honey and oligosaccharides.

label: Protein A / mAb quantification
reason: PDF guide describes Amber Protein A as a high-crosslinked PS/DVB Protein A column for rapid/accurate quantification of monoclonal antibody and Fc fusion proteins except IgG3.

label: HIC / ADC / protein fragments
reason: PDF guide describes Amber HIC Ethyl/Propyl/Butyl/Phenyl as HIC columns for mAbs, ADCs and protein fragments with different hydrophobicity.

label: Bio ion exchange
reason: PDF guide describes Amber SAX/WAX/SCX/WCX as polymer ion-exchange columns for proteins, cell lysate, nucleotides, polypeptides, carbohydrates/polysaccharides and nanoparticles.

label: Dedicated amino acids
reason: PDF guide lists Elite AAK/AAP dedicated amino acid columns for DNFB/DNPH and PITC derivatized amino acid analysis.

label: Melamine / dairy products
reason: PDF guide lists Elite MSP Tripolycyanamide column for determination of melamine/tripolycyanamide in dairy products.

label: Ginseng / ginsenosides
reason: PDF guide lists EliteUQ GIN C18 Ginseng column for determination of Ginsenosides Rg1 and Re in ginseng.

label: Carbamate pesticides
reason: PDF guide lists Elite ACO Carbamates Pesticides column for aldicarb, sodicarb, carboxanil and related carbamate pesticide analysis.

label: Phthalate plasticizers
reason: PDF guide lists Elite ROHS PAEs column for separation and analysis of phthalate plasticizers.

label: Beta-lactam antibiotics polymers
reason: PDF guide lists Sephadex G-10 dextran gel column for beta-lactam antibiotic polymer impurity analysis.

label: Vitamin K1 post-column reduction
reason: PDF guide lists EliteUG Zn Powder Reduction Column for Vitamin K1 post-column conversion to a fluorescent reduced product.

label: Organochlorine pesticides / cleanup
reason: PDF guide lists EliteUQ Bio-Beads S-X3/Amber S-X3 for 22 organochlorine pesticide residues, phthalic acid, antioxidants, semi-volatile organics and Sudan Red residues.

label: UHPLC AQ-C18 / biogenic amines
reason: PDF guide describes SinoPak HSS AQ-C18 as high-strength silica AQ-C18 compatible with 100% aqueous mobile phases and suitable for UHPLC, food additives and biogenic amines.

label: Cost-effective C18
reason: PDF guide describes Agressif C18 as ultra-pure silica C18 with high cost effectiveness.
